name: AuthorityCheck
description: A wrapper component that conditionally renders its children based on role-based access control. Compares user authorities against required authorities and only renders content when there's a match.
usage_guidelines: Use to protect UI elements based on user roles.
Wrap any component or content that should be role-restricted.
Pass the current user's roles via userAuthority prop.
Pass required roles via authority prop.
Returns null when no role match is found.

userAuthority.description: Array of role strings representing the current user's authorities/permissions.

authority.description: Array of role strings that are required to view the wrapped content.

children.description: Content to render when authority check passes.

styling_info.visual_description: This is a logical wrapper component with no visual styling. It simply renders or hides its children based on role matching.

Do: Use for hiding UI elements based on user roles
Combine with backend authorization for security
Pass accurate user roles from auth context
Use descriptive role names for clarity
Nest multiple AuthorityCheck components for complex permissions
Dont: Don't rely solely on this for security (always validate on backend)
Don't use empty authority arrays (renders children by default)
Don't use for non-role-based conditional rendering
Don't forget to handle loading states for user roles
